data.title: Pactiv Evergreen jumps on report of possible takeover by Apollo's Novolex
data.description:
Novolex, the packaging maker owned by private-equity firm Apollo Global Management (APO), is weighing a takeover of Pactiv Evergreen (NASDAQ:PTVE), Bloomberg News reported Tuesday, citing people familiar with the matter.

Shares of Pactiv Evergreen (PTVE) jumped as much as 18%.

Novolex is in talks with financial advisers about a potential bid, and no final decision has been made, Bloomberg News reported, citing its unnamed sources.

data.title: Agco rated Equal Weight in new research coverage at Morgan Stanley
data.description:
Agco (NYSE:AGCO) on Tuesday was rated Equal Weight in new research coverage by analysts at financial-services firm Morgan Stanley. They said the maker of farming machinery is fairly valued amid signs of soft demand in the key North American market.

“Heading into the second year of a global ag market downturn, we see a mixed fundamental picture for shares of Agco (AGCO),” Angel Castillo, analyst at Morgan Stanley, said in a December 3 report. “We can't rule out a longer or more pronounced earnings trough at Agco (AGCO) versus peers due to its positioning in North America where it has a relatively higher level of inventory overhang and a tougher road ahead.”

Morgan Stanley set a 12-month price target of $101 a share for Agco (AGCO), based on an estimated multiple of about 17 times next-12-month earnings.

“The market remains generally bearish on the global ag equipment cycle and Agco’s (AGCO) valuation gap to Deere (DE) suggests that it is already pricing in the risk of underperformance,” according to Morgan Stanley. “As such, we see a relatively balanced risk/reward for shares of Agco (AGCO) from here.”

Agco's equipment brands include Massey Ferguson, Challenger, Fendt, Valtra and Grain & Protein.

Agco's (AGCO) stock has declined about 17% since the beginning of the year, contrasting with the 27% gain for the Standard & Poor's 500 stock index (SP500).

data.title: Avadel chief commercial officer resigns, stock down 14%
data.description:
Shares of Avadel Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:AVDL) fell 14% Tuesday morning after the company disclosed its chief commercial officer, Richard Kim, was stepping down on Dec. 31.

Avadel (AVDL) said in a filing made early Tuesday that Kim resigned from the company and that the parties have agreed to a “form of separation and release agreement consistent with the terms of his employment agreement.” It added that Kim will also be eligible for 2024 incentive compensation.

Last month, Avadel (AVDL) reported a narrowed Q3 loss on substantially higher net revenue, beating Street estimates.

data.title: Ventas tenant Brookdale fails to exercise lease extension
data.description:
Ventas (NYSE:VTR) said on Tuesday that Brookdale Senior Living (NYSE:BKD) didn't exercise its right to extend the term of its master lease for renewal starting Jan. 1, 2026.

That means Brookdale (NYSE:BKD) no longer has the right to extend the lease term for any assets currently covered by the master lease. There are 120 senior housing communities currently covered by the master lease. Annual cash rent under the lease 2025 is $113.6M.

As a result, Ventas (NYSE:VTR) expects to use its Oi platform to convert some or all of the senior housing communities covered by the lease to Ventas's senior housing operating portfolio ("SHOP") structure and find market-focused operators to manage the communities.

The company plans to further expand its SHOP footprint to increase Ventas’s (VTR) future growth rate amid expected strong demand due from a large and growing aging population, it said.

Ventas (VTR) may also sell, lease, or take other actions regarding a portion of the currently leased portfolio.

Brookdale (BKD) remains obligated to pay full contractual rent under the master lease through the current lease term, which ends Dec. 31, 2025.

Ventas (VTR) stock rose 0.7% and Brookdale (BKD) stock gained 0.9% in Tuesday morning trading.

data.title: Top quant-rated Cyclical Stocks, as group is expected to outperform in 2025
data.description:
U.S. cyclicals should outperform in 2025, according to BofA Securities analysts.

We are in a post-peak inflation of 9% in 2022 but in a higher rate regime than the 2010s, and today’s environment looks a lot like the late 90s to early 2000s, as just a few large expensive stocks make up a third of the S&P 500 (SP500).

Savita Subramanian, head of U.S. equity and quantitative strategy at BofA, said she sees more opportunities in the average stock than in the index. “We like companies with healthy cash return prospects and a tether to the U.S. economy – large cap value stocks.”

She forecasts $275 in 2025 EPS, up 13% year-over-year compared to 10% expected in 2024.

“Volume is the story that replaces the last two years mega-cap tech story,” wrote Thomas Thornton, head of Global Research Product Marketing, in a note.

In addition, recovery in manufacturing should drive a 6% pickup in sales growth, driving operational leverage in cyclical sectors. Also, “2026 will likely be a year of normalization with profits growth closing in on the long-term average of 7%.”

De-regulation is also expected to benefit cyclicals. Thornton writes that regulations after 2008 prevented financials’ (XLF) multiples from expanding and stalled the public banks’ loan growth.

Also, analysts believe that the de-stocking cycle has come to an end.

“Semis (SMH), (SOXX) and autos saw the biggest decline in inventories year-over-year, while pharma (PJP), (XPH), (PPH), biotech (XBI), (BBH), and life sciences and consumer durables and apparel saw the biggest increase,” Thornton said.

After prior destocking troughs, such as in 2003, 2001, and 2022, S&P 500 (SP500) earnings grew 6%, 44%, and 35% (respectively) in the subsequent 12 months.

Two other factors influencing analysts being bullish cyclicals are the election uncertainty being over, and the S&P 500 (SP500) capex cycle inflecting higher, up 17% year-over-year in the third quarter – the strongest growth rate since the third quarter of 2022.

“More importantly, the S&P 500 ex-Mag. 7 and Tech started to see accelerating capex growth for the first time since 3Q22, growing at 7% YoY (vs. +2% YoY),” Thornton said. “Accelerating capex growth from non-Tech sectors suggests fundamental strength potentially broadening out to more picks and shovels from AI investments.”

Lastly, analysts expect S&P 500 (SP500) earnings to broaden out. EPS growth in the second half of next year is expected to see a record earnings breadth of 96%.

BofA analysts are overweight financials (XLF), materials (XLB), real estate (XLRE), utilities (XLU), and consumer discretionary (XLY).

Top-rated cyclicals, ranked by Seeking Alpha quant metrics:

  • Kingstone Companies Inc. (KINS) – Quant rating: 4.99

data.title: Bristol in research agreement with AI Proteins on miniprotein-based therapies
data.description:
• Privately held AI Proteins has signed a research collaboration agreement with Bristol Myers Squibb (NYSE:BMY) to utilize the former's platform to discover novel miniprotein-based therapeutics.
  • AI Proteins has an AI-based platform that can design miniproteins against targets chosen by Bristol. AI Proteins says that their miniproteins, "characterized by their small size and high affinity, have the potential to offer significant advantages over traditional antibody-based therapies, including improved tissue penetration and rapid clearance."
  • Terms of the deal call for an unspecified upfront payment with AI Proteins responsible for developing miniprotein for two undisclosed targets.
  • Bristol also has an option for an exclusive worldwide license to the miniproteins and two more target options in exchange for an expansion fee and potential milestone payments of up to $400M plus royalties on sales.

data.title: South Korean won plummets against the U.S. Dollar after President declares emergency
data.description:
South Korea’s won fell dramatically against the U.S. dollar on Tuesday, after the South Korean President, Yoon Suk Yeol declared a state of emergency and martial law in a sudden late night address to the country.

The U.S. Dollar (USD:KRW) was last seen trading up more than 2% against the South Korean currency, which touched a two-year low on the announcement.

In an unannounced address broadcast live on the country’s YTN television, Yoon attacked his domestic political opponents, and claimed that we would eradicate "shameless pro-North Korean anti-state forces".

Yoon did not mention any specific danger from the neighboring North Korea, which has lately been a nuclear threat to the country.

Other South Korean assets also tumbled on the news, as investors grew concerned about political turmoil and unrest in the country. The iShares MSCI South Korea ETF (EWY) fell more than 6% in US trading, its worst intraday fall since August.

Several other ETFs also slumped on the announcement, such as the Franklin FTSE South Korea ETF (FLKR) which dropped more than 5.6%, while Direxion Daily South Korea Bull 3X Shares ETF (KORU) fell more than 20%.

U.S. listed shares of Korea-based companies also reacted sharply. Shares of e-commerce company Coupang (CPNG) fell more than 5.5%, steel processor Posco Holdings (PKX) fell as much as 6.5%, while shares of KB Financial Group (KB) also dropped more than 5%.

"I declare martial law to protect the free Republic of Korea from the threat of North Korean communist forces, to eradicate the despicable pro-North Korean anti-state forces that are plundering the freedom and happiness of our people, and to protect the free constitutional order," Yoon said.

Yoon’s surprise address shocked the country. He said he had no choice but to resort to such a measure to safeguard constitutional order, claiming that the opposition parties have taken hostage of the parliamentary process.

data.title: 15 high flyer stocks that have soared over 100% in 2024 that still offer potential growth
data.description:
Investors are always on the lookout for high-flyer stocks that have experienced significant valuation increases and are offering potential for continued growth and profit.

As a result, Seeking Alpha constructed a list of high flyer stocks that may be of interest to traders and investors on Wall Street. The list of companies that were screened all hold a Buy or Strong Buy rating from SA quant metrics, have at least doubled year to date (up 100%), and all names each have a forward P/E under 30 which to many would be classified as reasonably cheap from a valuation stance.

The top 15 names are sorted below by year-to-date performance.

data.title: CVS Health upgraded to buy from hold by Deutsche Bank
data.description:
Deutsche Bank has upgraded CVS Health (NYSE:CVS) to buy from hold, asserting that the company’s earnings and business multiple are "now likely near trough levels" while it has taken measures to improve profitability.

The investment bank also said it conducted an analysis of CVS’s (CVS) managed care business and “believes the earnings recovery is both achievable and will outpace Street expectations.”

Other factors contributing to the upgrade were a recent change in management, continued strength in the company’s prescription benefits management services, a “measured decline in pharmacy and a compelling risk/reward thesis,” according to the bank’s note.

Deutsche Bank also adjusted its EPS estimates, lowering its 2024 estimate by 41% to $5.15 versus the consensus $5.35, but raising its 2025 estimate by 22% to $6.27 versus the consensus $6.18 “as we model a slow recovery in the managed care segment.”

The investment bank maintained its price target of $66.

data.title: Lisata Therapeutics and Kuva Labs announce global license agreement for solid tumor imaging
data.description:
• Lisata Therapeutics (NASDAQ:LSTA) and Kuva Labs, a wholly owned subsidiary of Mi2 Holdings, announced a global collaboration and license agreement.
  • Under the terms of this agreement, Kuva gains access to Lisata's iRGD cyclic peptide product candidate, certepetide, as a targeting and enhanced delivery agent to be used with Kuva’s NanoMark platform technology.
  • Kuva will assume full responsibility for all research, development, and commercialization costs for NanoMark, while Lisata will be responsible for supplying certepetide.
  • The agreement includes an upfront license fee, as well as potential substantial milestone payments and royalties on future product sales to Lisata.
  • LSTA shares were up 2.4% on Tuesday.
  • Source: Press Release
